notes: tier 1 = computable now from data already in the project (existing Binance feed, existing indicator engine)
tier 2 = computable via a new free/public API integration
tier 3 = requires a paid data provider, or has no reliable public data source at all
Treat tier tags as a first-pass starting point, not gospel -- verify each as you build.

Fear & Greed Index appears in both 'market_sentiment' and 'social_media' categories, referencing the same underlying index (alternative.me). Combined effective weight from this one input is 3.00% + 0.50% = 3.50%. Confirm this double-count is intentional before building.

sub_factors.name: Order Book Imbalance
sub_factors.source: Binance order book depth (bids/asks) -- same exchange already used for price feed

sub_factors.name: Market Buy vs Sell Volume
sub_factors.source: Binance aggTrade stream, isBuyerMaker flag

sub_factors.name: Trade Volume
sub_factors.source: Binance kline/trade volume, already available

sub_factors.name: Liquidity Walls
sub_factors.source: Derived from order book depth -- large resting size clusters

sub_factors.name: Volume Delta
sub_factors.source: Cumulative buy volume minus sell volume from aggTrade stream

sub_factors.name: Volume Profile
sub_factors.source: Derived from trade data at each price level over a lookback window

sub_factors.name: VWAP Position
sub_factors.source: Already built (existing VWAP indicator) -- price relative to VWAP

sub_factors.name: Bid-Ask Spread
sub_factors.source: Binance order book best bid/ask

sub_factors.name: Market Depth / Slippage
sub_factors.source: Estimated slippage for a hypothetical order size from order book depth

sub_factors.name: Hidden Liquidity (Icebergs)
sub_factors.source: No reliable direct detection method. Heuristic proxy only (repeated replenishment at a price level) -- not a real detector, label it as such in UI

sub_factors.name: Fear & Greed Index
sub_factors.source: alternative.me free public API, no key required

sub_factors.name: Bullish vs Bearish Ratio
sub_factors.source: No canonical index exists; proxy via Binance Futures top-trader long/short account ratio, label as proxy

sub_factors.name: Retail FOMO
sub_factors.source: No canonical metric; weak proxy only from volume acceleration + price rate-of-change

sub_factors.name: Market Confidence
sub_factors.source: Vague construct, no direct source; weak proxy from realized volatility trend + funding rate stability

sub_factors.name: Panic Selling
sub_factors.source: Reasonably well-defined heuristic: volume spike concurrent with sharp price decline, computable from existing price/volume data

sub_factors.name: Trend Momentum
sub_factors.source: Standard momentum/ROC calculation from existing price data

sub_factors.name: Volatility Sentiment
sub_factors.source: Derived from ATR / realized volatility, already available

sub_factors.name: Market Recovery Confidence
sub_factors.source: Vague construct; weak proxy from higher-low structure after a drawdown

sub_factors.name: Open Interest
sub_factors.source: Binance Futures OI endpoint, free, new integration

sub_factors.name: Funding Rate
sub_factors.source: Binance Futures funding rate endpoint, free

sub_factors.name: Long/Short Ratio
sub_factors.source: Binance Futures top-trader long/short ratio endpoint, free

sub_factors.name: Liquidation Clusters
sub_factors.source: Binance Futures forceOrder liquidation stream, free; needs aggregation logic to find clusters

sub_factors.name: Leverage Ratio
sub_factors.source: No direct market-wide leverage metric published; weak proxy from OI change vs price change

sub_factors.name: Options Gamma Exposure
sub_factors.source: Requires BTC options chain data (Deribit is the standard source) plus Greeks calculation -- substantial new integration

sub_factors.name: Futures Basis
sub_factors.source: Futures price minus spot price, both already from Binance

sub_factors.name: Options Put/Call Ratio
sub_factors.source: Same Deribit options integration as Gamma Exposure

sub_factors.name: BTC Dominance %
sub_factors.source: CoinGecko free /global endpoint

sub_factors.name: Altcoin Rotation
sub_factors.source: Derivable from CoinGecko top-coin market caps vs BTC over time, needs a defined basket

sub_factors.name: ETH Dominance
sub_factors.source: Same CoinGecko /global endpoint

sub_factors.name: Stablecoin Dominance
sub_factors.source: Same CoinGecko /global endpoint (stablecoin market cap share)

sub_factors.name: BTC Momentum
sub_factors.source: Price rate of change, already have price data

sub_factors.name: Sector Rotation
sub_factors.source: Needs categorized coin sector data; low weight for the integration effort, consider deprioritizing

sub_factors.name: Interest Rates
sub_factors.source: FRED (Federal Reserve Economic Data) API, free but new integration, infrequent updates

sub_factors.name: CPI Inflation
sub_factors.source: FRED API, monthly release

sub_factors.name: FOMC Decisions
sub_factors.source: Check whether the existing 'FOMC Meeting' countdown already in the AI Summary card has a data source behind it -- may already be partially solved

sub_factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
sub_factors.source: Free financial data API (e.g. stooq, twelve data free tier) -- new integration outside crypto exchanges

sub_factors.name: Bond Yields
sub_factors.source: FRED API (10Y yield), infrequent updates

sub_factors.name: GDP Growth
sub_factors.source: FRED API, quarterly -- very low signal value at this update frequency

sub_factors.name: Employment Data
sub_factors.source: FRED/BLS API, monthly

sub_factors.name: Gold & Oil Trends
sub_factors.source: Free commodity price feed via a financial data API

sub_factors.name: Exchange Deposits
sub_factors.source: Needs on-chain analytics with labeled exchange wallets (CryptoQuant/Glassnode, mostly paid)

sub_factors.name: Exchange Withdrawals
sub_factors.source: Same as Exchange Deposits

sub_factors.name: Whale Wallet Transfers
sub_factors.source: Whale Alert API has a limited free tier, full access is paid

sub_factors.name: Large Orders
sub_factors.source: Large orders on the exchange order book/tape -- different from on-chain whales, computable from existing Binance data

sub_factors.name: OTC Transactions
sub_factors.source: Not observable via any public data source -- recommend excluding entirely rather than faking it

sub_factors.name: Institutional Wallets
sub_factors.source: Needs labeled wallet data (Arkham/Nansen), paid

sub_factors.name: Miner Wallet Activity
sub_factors.source: On-chain miner flow data, mostly paid (CryptoQuant); free approximations are low quality

sub_factors.name: USDT Flow
sub_factors.source: Needs labeled exchange wallets to measure 'flow,' not just mint/burn -- typically paid data providers

sub_factors.name: USDC Flow
sub_factors.source: Same as USDT Flow

sub_factors.name: Stablecoin Minting
sub_factors.source: Mint events are on-chain contract calls, trackable free via Etherscan/Tronscan APIs

sub_factors.name: Stablecoin Burning
sub_factors.source: Same, burn events trackable free via Etherscan/Tronscan

sub_factors.name: Exchange Inflows
sub_factors.source: Needs labeled exchange wallets

sub_factors.name: Exchange Outflows
sub_factors.source: Same as Exchange Inflows

sub_factors.name: Spot ETF Net Flow
sub_factors.source: Farside Investors publishes free daily BTC ETF flow data (no official API, but data is public and fetchable) -- verify it's still being published before relying on it

sub_factors.name: Institutional Buying
sub_factors.source: No real-time source; mostly inferred from lagged 13F filings

sub_factors.name: Institutional Selling
sub_factors.source: Same as Institutional Buying

sub_factors.name: Corporate Treasury Purchases
sub_factors.source: News-driven, not a clean API -- could route through the News/Regulations AI classification pipeline instead

sub_factors.name: Hedge Fund Positioning
sub_factors.source: CFTC Commitment of Traders report for CME BTC futures is a real free weekly data source

sub_factors.name: Government Holdings
sub_factors.source: No real-time source; occasional news/on-chain seizure tracking only

sub_factors.name: Trend (EMA/SMA)
sub_factors.source: Already built

sub_factors.name: RSI
sub_factors.source: Already built

sub_factors.name: MACD
sub_factors.source: Already built

sub_factors.name: Support & Resistance
sub_factors.source: Reuse existing antigravity zone engine

sub_factors.name: Bollinger Bands
sub_factors.source: Already built

sub_factors.name: ATR
sub_factors.source: Already built

sub_factors.name: ADX
sub_factors.source: Standard calculation from existing OHLC data, add if not already present

sub_factors.name: Fibonacci
sub_factors.source: Reuse existing Fibonacci drawing tool logic

sub_factors.name: Ichimoku
sub_factors.source: Standard calculation from existing OHLC data, add if not already present

sub_factors.name: Exchange Reserves
sub_factors.source: Glassnode/CryptoQuant, mostly paid; no clean free API

sub_factors.name: Active Addresses
sub_factors.source: Glassnode paid; free approximations are lower quality

sub_factors.name: MVRV Ratio
sub_factors.source: Glassnode-style metric, mostly paid

sub_factors.name: NVT Ratio
sub_factors.source: Computable yourself: market cap (CoinGecko, free) divided by on-chain transaction volume (mempool.space/blockchain.info, free)

sub_factors.name: Hash Rate
sub_factors.source: mempool.space or blockchain.info free API

sub_factors.name: Mining Difficulty
sub_factors.source: Same free sources as Hash Rate

sub_factors.name: Whale Wallet Growth
sub_factors.source: Needs labeled whale wallet tracking, paid

sub_factors.name: Realized Price
sub_factors.source: Complex on-chain calculation, typically a paid Glassnode metric

categories.name: News & Regulations
categories.build_note: All sub-factors in this category are fundamentally an NLP/classification task rather than a numeric API. Recommend: pull recent headlines from a free/cheap news API (e.g. CryptoPanic free tier), feed them through the existing AI analysis engine with a classification prompt per sub-factor, and score aggregate sentiment/impact 0-100. Build this pipeline once, reuse it for all 8 rows below and for Reddit Sentiment in social_media.

sub_factors.name: Government Regulations
sub_factors.source: News API + AI classification pipeline

sub_factors.name: ETF Approval/Rejection
sub_factors.source: News API + AI classification pipeline

sub_factors.name: Exchange Hacks
sub_factors.source: News API + AI classification pipeline

sub_factors.name: Exchange Listings/Delistings
sub_factors.source: News API + AI classification pipeline

sub_factors.name: Security Vulnerabilities
sub_factors.source: News API + AI classification pipeline

sub_factors.name: Country Adoption
sub_factors.source: News API + AI classification pipeline

sub_factors.name: Tax Policies
sub_factors.source: News API + AI classification pipeline

sub_factors.name: Major Partnerships
sub_factors.source: News API + AI classification pipeline

sub_factors.name: Fear & Greed Index
sub_factors.source: Same alternative.me API as market_sentiment's Fear & Greed Index -- reuse the same fetch, don't call it twice

sub_factors.name: X (Twitter) Sentiment
sub_factors.source: X API v2 meaningful search access is paid/restricted -- verify current pricing/access before committing

sub_factors.name: Reddit Sentiment
sub_factors.source: Reddit API is free with app registration (rate-limited); route through the AI classification pipeline built for news_regulations

sub_factors.name: Google Trends
sub_factors.source: Free via unofficial pytrends library, but fragile and rate-limited -- treat as best-effort

sub_factors.name: YouTube Influence
sub_factors.source: No clean metric without a curated channel-tracking system -- low value for the effort at this weight

sub_factors.name: Telegram/Discord Activity
sub_factors.source: No public aggregate data source without scraping specific servers -- recommend excluding

sub_factors.name: Influencer Impact
sub_factors.source: Fundamentally unmeasurable without a curated influencer list -- recommend excluding
